stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats parental alienation as a real and assessable phenomenon; operates or endorses PA-specific intervention / alienating-behaviour expert-witness work
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues the PA construct is misused in family court, especially against domestic-abuse survivors; advocates trauma-informed alternatives and tighter regulation of PA experts
stance_taxonomy.middle: engages both alienating-behaviour and family-violence frames; works within FJC / BPS / AFCC-aligned differential-diagnosis approach

regulatory_context.leading_case: Re Y (Experts and Alienating Behaviour: The Modern Approach) [2026] EWFC 38, Sir Andrew McFarlane P, 20 Feb 2026
regulatory_context.ruling_summary: Permission should not be given for the instruction of an expert 'psychologist' who is neither registered by a relevant statutory body, nor chartered by the BPS.
regulatory_context.key_quote_fjc_2024_para_2: 'Parental alienation syndrome' has no evidential basis and is considered a harmful pseudo-science.

practitioners.name: Karen Woodall
practitioners.practice: Co-founder and lead therapist, Family Separation Clinic (London); Karen Woodall Trainings
uk_registration: BACP-sanctioned 2015 for professional malpractice; sanction lifted May 2016
practitioners.specialty: Court-instructed assessment and therapeutic-parenting intervention in alienation cases; international PA practitioner training
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.familyseparationclinic.com/ — clinic framing of PA as discrete assessable treatable phenomenon; https://transparencyproject.org.uk/parental-alienation-experts/
programs_or_publications: Co-author Understanding Parental Alienation (Charles C. Thomas, 2017, with Nick Woodall)
practitioners.hook: Single most-named UK PA practitioner; unavoidable for any UK recognition-camp directory and equally unavoidable for the critique camp's regulatory case post-Re Y.
practitioners.notes: Unregulated practitioner; post-Re Y framework means she should not be instructed as psychologist expert in future cases. 2015 BACP sanction (lacked Sincerity, Integrity, Competence and Wisdom) disclosed at http://thecustodyminefield.com/karen-woodall-bacp-sanction-compliance/.

practitioners.name: Nick Woodall
practitioners.practice: Co-founder and partner, Family Separation Clinic LLP; independent psychotherapist
uk_registration: Therapeutic-mediator accreditation (School of Psychotherapy & Counselling Psychology, Regents University London)
HCPC / BPS chartered status not publicly confirmed
practitioners.specialty: Direct therapeutic work with separated families; co-trainer FSC PA practitioner training
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.familyseparationclinic.com/ — co-author of clinic's PA model; co-author Understanding Parental Alienation (2017)
programs_or_publications: Co-author Understanding Parental Alienation (Charles C. Thomas, 2017)
Co-trainer on FSC's international PA practitioner training
practitioners.hook: Co-architect of the dominant UK PA-recognition clinical model; useful as the 'other Woodall' for press symmetry.
practitioners.notes: Same Re Y regulatory exposure as Karen Woodall.

practitioners.name: Dr Sue Whitcombe, DCounsPsy, CPsychol, AFBPsS
practitioners.practice: Principal Psychologist and founder, Family Psychology Solutions CIC (Teesside University-supported)
uk_registration: BPS Chartered Member and Associate Fellow (CPsychol, AFBPsS)
Deputy Chair BPS Training Committee for Counselling Psychology
practitioners.specialty: Court-instructed assessment in alienation / implacable hostility cases; targeted evidence-informed intervention; training for legal, mental-health and social-work professions on PA
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.bps.org.uk/psychologist/new-voices-parental-alienation-time-notice-time-intervene ; https://www.bps.org.uk/psychologist/parental-alienation-time-make-difference ; PhD thesis Cardiff Metropolitan 'Powerlessness, betrayal, abuse: The trauma of parental alienation'
programs_or_publications: Family Psychology Solutions CIC clinical service
BPS Expert Witness Advisory Group member
practitioners.hook: The only UK CPsychol + HCPC-registered counselling psychologist with sustained PA-specific publication record; survives Re Y regulatory test on the recognition side.
practitioners.notes: Distinguished from the Woodalls precisely because she meets McFarlane P's regulatory bar.

practitioners.name: Dr Adrienne Barnett
practitioners.practice: Reader in Law, Brunel Law School, Brunel University London; former family-law barrister
practitioners.specialty: Empirical/doctrinal critique of PA as courtroom rhetorical device weaponised against DV survivors
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09649069.2020.1701950 — Barnett (2020) A genealogy of hostility, JSWFL 42(1):18-29
programs_or_publications: BA/Leverhulme-funded action research 'Domestic Abuse, Parental Alienation and the Family Courts' (2022-2025)
Co-editor JSWFL Festschrift for Felicity Kaganas (2024)
Advisory roles: Rights of Women; Cafcass Research Advisory Committee; SHERA co-founder; Hague Mothers UK lead
practitioners.hook: Single most-cited UK academic critique of PA-as-rhetoric; her 2020 paper is the entry point to the entire UK critique-camp literature.
practitioners.notes: Already in /practitioners/lawyers/uk; this entry focuses on research-practice rather than bar credentials.

practitioners.name: Professor Felicity Kaganas
practitioners.practice: Professor, Brunel Law School, Brunel University London
practitioners.specialty: Feminist socio-legal scholarship; critique of presumption of parental involvement and PA deployment against DV-survivor mothers
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09649069.2018.1519155 — Kaganas (2018) Introduction: contact and domestic abuse, JSWFL ; https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09649069.2024.2414627 — 2024 JSWFL Festschrift
practitioners.hook: Doyenne of UK feminist family-law scholarship; citing her unlocks the wider Brunel/UCL DV-academic network.
practitioners.notes: Cross-referenced in /practitioners/lawyers/uk; included here because she is regularly cited by therapists/psychologists in the critique camp.

practitioners.name: Dr Sonja Ayeb-Karlsson
practitioners.practice: Senior Lecturer / Researcher, UCL Risk & Disaster Reduction; UCL Everyday Disasters and Violences Research Group
practitioners.specialty: Lead author 2024 UCL three-case-study analysis of UK family-court cases where unregulated experts advised removal of children from DV-survivor mothers on basis of alienation
programs_or_publications: Ayeb-Karlsson (2024) JSWFL three-case-study of unregulated PA experts
UCL Everyday Disasters and Violences Research Group
practitioners.hook: Her three-case-study paper is the single best UK empirical artefact of harms caused by unregulated PA experts; directly cited in post-Re Y discourse.
practitioners.notes: Research-practitioner; entry included because the research she produced is the empirical underpinning of post-Re Y regulatory tightening.

practitioners.name: Dr Claire Sturge
practitioners.practice: Consultant Child & Adolescent Psychiatrist (retired NHS; long-standing Family Court expert witness)
uk_registration: Current registration status post-retirement not publicly confirmed
practitioners.specialty: Child psychiatric assessment in contact/residence disputes; co-author foundational UK court report on contact and DV
practitioners.stance_source: Sturge & Glaser (2000) 'Contact and Domestic Violence: The Experts' Court Report', Family Law (September) 615 — cited at footnote 2 of FJC 2024 Guidance as authority that PAS 'has no evidential basis'
programs_or_publications: Sturge & Glaser (2000) UK court canonical text
Repeated court-appointed expert in private-law children's cases
practitioners.primary_contact: Not publicly confirmed; route via Royal College of Psychiatrists CAP faculty
practitioners.hook: Sturge & Glaser 2000 is THE UK authority cited by FJC for rejection of PAS; naming her places directory on same evidence base courts now use.
practitioners.notes: Primarily historical/referent; contact gap noted honestly.

practitioners.name: Professor Danya Glaser
practitioners.practice: Visiting Professor, UCL Psychoanalysis Unit; Honorary Consultant Child & Adolescent Psychiatrist, Great Ormond Street Hospital
uk_registration: GMC-registered psychiatrist (historic; current status post-honorary not publicly confirmed)
practitioners.specialty: Child maltreatment, emotional abuse, fabricated/induced illness; child-protection expert witness; co-author Sturge & Glaser court report
practitioners.stance_source: Sturge & Glaser (2000); chair of NICE Development Group 'When to Suspect Child Maltreatment' (2009); former FJC member per UCL profile
practitioners.hook: UK's most-senior child-psychiatric voice rejecting PAS as diagnosable construct; bridges medical-establishment side of critique camp.
practitioners.notes: Retains honorary appointments; contact route is institutional.

practitioners.name: Dr Hannah Jones, ForenPsyD, CPsychol, AFBPsS
practitioners.practice: Senior Lecturer, Centre for Psychiatry & Mental Health, Queen Mary University of London; Programme Director MSc Forensic Psychology & Mental Health (BPS-accredited); independent forensic-psychology expert-witness practice
uk_registration: HCPC-registered Forensic Psychologist (PYL protected title; specific HCPC number not publicly confirmed)
Stage 2 Assessor for BPS Qualification in Forensic Psychology
practitioners.specialty: Trauma-informed and domestic-abuse-informed forensic assessment of parents in Family Court; alternative to PA-diagnosis expert pipeline
practitioners.stance_source: https://transparencyproject.org.uk/parental-alienation-experts/ — quoted: 'true alienation ... is rare enough that it does not need its own concept'
programs_or_publications: QMUL MSc Forensic Psychology & Mental Health (Programme Director)
Expert-witness reports in UK family proceedings (trauma-informed framework)
practitioners.hook: A regulated UK CPsychol who explicitly rejects standalone PA diagnosis; the critique-camp answer to 'who *should* the court be instructing?' post-Re Y.
practitioners.notes: Survives Re Y regulatory bar squarely and is publicly quoted opposing standalone-PA framing.

practitioners.name: Dr Jaime Craig, Consultant Clinical & Forensic Psychologist
practitioners.practice: Chair, Association of Clinical Psychologists UK (ACP-UK); member Family Justice Council; lead author 2022 BPS/FJC Guidance on Psychologists as Expert Witnesses
practitioners.specialty: Family-court expert-witness practice; regulation of psychologist experts; lead author of tightened framework now enforced by Re Y
practitioners.hook: Craig literally wrote the rules McFarlane P enforced in Re Y; cleanest UK source for 'what regulated practice looks like' in PA-allegation cases.
practitioners.notes: Sits at intersection of clinical leadership and FJC policymaking; uniquely placed for policy-press interviews.

practitioners.name: Dr Julie Doughty
practitioners.practice: Reader in Law, Cardiff University School of Law and Politics; lead author Cafcass Cymru-commissioned PA evidence review
practitioners.specialty: Evidence-base review of PA research and case law for Welsh Government / Cafcass Cymru; peer-reviewed work on professional responses to PA
practitioners.stance_source: https://www.gov.wales/sites/default/files/publications/2018-05/review-of-research-and-case-law-on-parental-alienation.pdf — Doughty, Maxwell & Slater (2018) ; https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/09649069.2020.1701938 — Doughty et al. (2020) JSWFL
programs_or_publications: Cited at footnote 2 of FJC 2024 Guidance
practitioners.hook: Her 2018 government-commissioned review is the canonical UK 'middle-ground' evidence document; cited by both camps and embedded in FJC 2024 Guidance.
practitioners.notes: Genuinely centrist; neither dismisses nor endorses PA as discrete phenomenon; valuable non-aligned interview source.

practitioners.name: Professor Marianne Hester, OBE PhD
practitioners.practice: Professor and Head, Centre for Gender and Violence Research, School for Policy Studies, University of Bristol
practitioners.specialty: DV research; Three-Planet Model (DV / private family law / child protection); co-author seminal post-separation contact / DV studies
practitioners.stance_source: Hester (2011) 'Three Planet Model', BJSW 41(5):837-853 ; Hester & Radford (1996) Mothering Through Domestic Violence
programs_or_publications: Centre for Gender and Violence Research (Bristol)
practitioners.hook: Hester's Three-Planet Model is the single most-cited UK framework for explaining family-court mishandling of DV/PA/safeguarding crossover; useful for press placement on structural problem.
practitioners.notes: Classified middle because primary frame is DV-safeguarding, not standalone-PA rejection; foundational to critique camp's case but not a PA practitioner per se.

excluded_practitioners.name: Melanie Gill
excluded_practitioners.reason: Unregulated; not HCPC-registered; not chartered by BPS; no clinical or therapeutic practice. McFarlane P in Re Y [2026] EWFC 38 (20 Feb 2026) set aside findings made on basis of her assessment and held going forward that 'permission should not be given for the instruction of an expert psychologist who is neither registered by a relevant statutory body, nor chartered by the BPS.'
excluded_practitioners.parallel_treatment: Direct UK parallel to US directory's exclusion of Randy Rand

borderline_or_not_verified.name: Dr Emma Katz
borderline_or_not_verified.actual_affiliation: Edge Hill University (NOT Birmingham City University as in source brief)
borderline_or_not_verified.reason: Pure academic researcher; no clinical/therapeutic practice; consider for /influencers/ instead

borderline_or_not_verified.name: Dr Karen Treisman MBE
borderline_or_not_verified.reason: HCPC-registered Clinical Psychologist (Safe Hands Thinking Minds) but no public PA-specific position or PA-case practice verifiable

borderline_or_not_verified.name: Dr Anne Lazenbatt
borderline_or_not_verified.reason: Strong DV/child-maltreatment record at QUB but no PA-specific publication or court testimony verifiable

borderline_or_not_verified.name: Professor Jane L. Ireland
borderline_or_not_verified.reason: HCPC-registered forensic psychologist with substantial Family-Court expert work; PA-specific stance not publicly confirmed; 2012 FJC-commissioned report is methodological not PA-specific

borderline_or_not_verified.name: Sue Atkins
borderline_or_not_verified.reason: UK parenting coach; not regulated clinician; no PA-specific public position verifiable

borderline_or_not_verified.name: Kayla Smith / Reignite Family Connections
borderline_or_not_verified.reason: Could not verify; no UK BACP-registered Kayla Smith / Reignite Family Connections in public records; the only verifiable Kayla Smith in PA-adjacent practice is US (Nashville MFT)

borderline_or_not_verified.name: Brian O'Sullivan
borderline_or_not_verified.reason: Ireland-based (Changes.ie, Cork); out of UK scope

honest_gaps: Recognition camp thin (3 entries) and dominated by FSC — reflects regulatory reality post-Re Y where most named UK PA experts are unregulated and now formally excluded from future Family Court instruction.
Middle camp thin (2 entries) — UK PA discourse is more polarised than US; no UK equivalent of AFCC OBFC/Drozd/Sullivan triangulation network.

Critique camp drew heavily on academic researchers (Barnett, Kaganas, Ayeb-Karlsson, Hester) plus medical-establishment psychiatrists (Sturge, Glaser) plus regulated psychologists (Hannah Jones, Jaime Craig) — reflecting post-Re Y institutional alignment with HCPC/BPS/FJC.
FJC December 2024 Guidance does not name individual working-group members beyond Sir Andrew McFarlane (Chair); group described only as multidisciplinary.
Northern Ireland / Scotland coverage essentially zero — all verified entries are England/Wales-based. Cafcass Cymru cited via Doughty's review; Scottish practitioners not surfaced.
